# `sesam hook post-checkout` gets git's is-branch-checkout flag as its 3rd arg.
# A branch switch ("1", also clone) aggressively drops stale plaintext under
# .sesam before revealing; a file checkout ("0", `git checkout -- path`) must
# only re-reveal and never wipe the worktree.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
cp secret.env app.env
exec sesam add app.env --group admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam with a secret'

# Absent plaintext (so we can observe the reveal) plus an untracked file that a
# clean would remove.
rm app.env
cp junk keep.txt

# File checkout (flag 0): reveals, but must NOT clean the untracked file.
exec sesam hook post-checkout prev new 0
exists app.env
exists keep.txt

# Branch checkout (flag 1): aggressively cleans stale untracked files under
# .sesam, then re-reveals the current secrets.
exec sesam hook post-checkout prev new 1
! exists keep.txt
exists app.env
grep 'super-secret' app.env

-- secret.env --
TOKEN=super-secret
-- junk --
untracked leftover
